scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented server-side v2 REST endpoint (Rooms, Sessions, active-rooms, Recordings, Recording-assets, Live-streams, External-streams, Room-codes, Templates/Policy, Polls, Analytics) is enumerated below. 8 read streams (4 wave2 legacy-parity + 4 new: live_streams, external_streams, recording_assets, webhook_events) and 7 write actions (create_room, update_room, create_template, create_room_code, update_room_code, start_recording, stop_recording) are now covered. Peer-level real-time control (active-rooms peer list/get/update/remove/message, room end), analytics event-detail endpoints, live/external-stream lifecycle mutations, poll-response reads, and recording-asset presigned-URL/binary retrieval remain out of scope per the categories below.

excluded.reason: single-room detail subsumed by the rooms list stream; no distinct fields beyond what /v2/rooms already returns per record
single-session detail subsumed by the sessions list stream
single-recording detail subsumed by the recordings list stream

single-recording-id variant of stop_recording (room-scoped stop is the covered, more commonly used shape); adding a second near-identical write action for the same mutation is not a distinct capability
recording-pause lifecycle control; deferred pending real usage demand for pause/resume beyond start/stop
recording-resume lifecycle control; paired with the deferred pause action above
single-asset detail subsumed by the recording_assets list stream
returns a signed URL for downloading the underlying recording media file, not syncable catalog/object data
single-live-stream detail subsumed by the live_streams list stream
HLS live-stream lifecycle start requires a real active room and destination config with no read-side parity gap; deferred until a Pass B write-review pass covers streaming-control actions
paired lifecycle-stop for the deferred live-stream start action above
single-id variant of the room-scoped stop action above
live-stream recording pause; deferred with the other live-stream lifecycle actions
live-stream recording resume; deferred with the other live-stream lifecycle actions
injects timed metadata into an active HLS manifest; a real-time broadcast control action, not a syncable-record mutation
single-external-stream detail subsumed by the external_streams list stream
RTMP push-to-destination lifecycle start; deferred with the other streaming-control actions pending Pass B write review
paired lifecycle-stop for the deferred external-stream start action above
single-id variant of the room-scoped stop action above

room-codes are per-room join-authentication credentials, not a general syncable object stream; listing them at scale would require fanning out over every room_id and expose active join secrets in warehouse-destined data
single-template detail subsumed by the templates list stream

full template update (roles/settings/destinations sub-objects) is a large nested-object POST-as-PATCH surface; only template creation is covered, matching the create-first parity most connectors of this shape ship
role detail is already embedded in each template list-stream record's roles map
per-role policy sub-object mutation; deferred with the full template-update action above
deletes a role from a template's policy, potentially breaking any room still assigning peers that role
settings sub-object is already embedded in each template list-stream record
settings sub-object mutation; deferred with the full template-update action above
destinations sub-object is already embedded in each template list-stream record
destinations sub-object mutation; deferred with the full template-update action above
live in-memory active-room/session snapshot, not a syncable catalog object; equivalent information is available via the sessions stream's active filter once a session has been recorded
ephemeral live-peer roster of a currently active room; ceases to exist once the room session ends
single ephemeral live-peer detail; same reasoning as the peers list above
mutates a live participant's role/mute state in an in-progress call
forcibly disconnects live participants from an in-progress call
sends a real-time broadcast/direct message into an active room; a live-interaction side-effect, not a data-object mutation with a record shape to validate
forcibly terminates a live in-progress room session for all participants
poll creation requires an active room's live poll-engine state; no corresponding read stream exists yet to establish parity against
poll detail/results read; no poll-listing endpoint exists in the documented API to build a catalog stream from (polls are only retrievable by ID or via room linkage)
individual poll response records; same reasoning as poll detail above
links a poll to a room; a session-setup action tied to the deferred polls feature above

raw track/recording/error telemetry event stream distinct from webhook-delivery history; a large, high-cardinality diagnostic firehose better suited to a dedicated observability pipeline than a warehouse sync catalog
per-peer call-quality telemetry (packet loss/jitter/bitrate time series); same high-cardinality diagnostic-data reasoning as analytics/events
re-triggers delivery of a historical webhook event to the configured endpoint; an operational replay action, not a data read/create
creates a fresh RTMP ingest stream key/secret for a room; a credential-issuance action out of scope for a reverse-ETL write surface
retrieves the live RTMP ingest stream key/secret for a room; a credential value, not syncable catalog data
revokes an active RTMP ingest stream key, breaking any in-progress ingest using it
